fields.description:
Provide an explicit capture helper that lets agents record verbose session logs
without hand-assembling `create` + `comment` calls — one call that creates a
`session_log` on first use and appends an entry — available from all three
interfaces.

## Why

Beyond plain `create` / `edit` / `comment`, agents benefit from a single
convenience call that creates the session log (if needed) and appends activity to
it, so verbose logging is low-friction and consistent. It is thin sugar over the
existing shared write path, so it flows to library, CLI, and MCP uniformly.

## What

- A core helper that resolves/creates the current `session_log` (carrying a short
  work-summary title) and appends an entry as a COMMENT, via the existing
  `composer.create_core` + `leaf.comment` write path.
- Library: `rebar.append_session_log(entry, *, summary=None)`.
- CLI: `rebar session-log append "<entry>"` (and a `start --summary` to create the
  log explicitly).
- MCP: a write-gated `log_session(entry)` tool (no-op/refused under
  `REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1`).
- Allow optional `relates_to` / `discovered_from` links from the log to the work
  it documents.

## Scope

Explicit, agent-invoked capture helper only. Relies on the foundation (the type +
write-path rules). An automatic per-session hook is **explicitly out of scope** for
this epic (no honest cross-interface trigger — the CLI has no long-lived process —
and it introduces write amplification).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A capture helper can create a `session_log` and append entries to it from the
      library, the CLI, and MCP, all through the single shared write path.
- [ ] First use creates exactly one log (with the summary title); subsequent calls
      append rather than recreating.
- [ ] Logs may carry `relates_to` / `discovered_from` links; blocking links remain
      refused.
- [ ] The MCP tool is refused/no-op under `REBAR_MCP_READONLY=1`.
- [ ] Tests cover create-then-append and the three-interface parity.
fields.title: Session-log capture helper (explicit, all three interfaces)
